permanent: When running Python one-liners with -c, use double quotes for the code and avoid spaces after 'import'.
Use cmd.exe or PowerShell for shell commands; Unix commands like pwd and ls fail on Windows.
mkdir -p and mkdir fail if directory already exists on Windows; use 'if not exist' or Python os.makedirs.
Use sys.executable instead of 'python' to invoke Python in subprocess on Windows.
Avoid relying on shell-builtins; prefer Python code for file operations like removing __pycache__.

temporary.text: On Windows, printing Unicode characters (e.g., \u2713) may cause UnicodeEncodeError; avoid non-ASCII in console output.
